answer:
\boxed{B}

Repeated fracture of a porcelain-fused-to-metal (PFM) restoration is most commonly caused by an **inadequately designed framework**, which fails to provide sufficient structural support or proper stress distribution. Poor framework design (e.g., thin sections, sharp angles, or improper contour) leads to excessive stress concentration during function, causing porcelain delamination or metal framework breakage. While occlusal trauma (A) can contribute, it typically exacerbates an underlying design flaw rather than being the primary cause.
